metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A Gnomish city where Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors, Healing Hall Beacon, Hanging Charms Veiled High Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Hearthglen should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Hearthglen organizes power through Hanging Charms Veiled High Court, public response through Healing Hall Beacon, civic memory through Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors, and daily life through S1 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S2 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Low Residential Civic Hall, S5 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Granite, Peat Brick, Oak, Copper, Dyed Wool, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Ward Networks, Ritual Geometry, Dream Binding, and exact anchors including Hanging Charms Veiled High Court, Healing Hall Beacon, Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors, Ancestor Vault, Alchemy Lab, Tenement Court, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A Gnomish civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors, Healing Hall Beacon, Hanging Charms Veiled High Court, and section anchors such as Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, Civic Services Hall, Low Residential Civic Hall, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Hearthglen uses city code HEA.
The Wonder is Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors.
The Beacon is Healing Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Hanging Charms Veiled High Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.

The source-backed section spine is: S1 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S2 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Low Residential Civic Hall, S5 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Hearthglen as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Hearthglen's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

skyline: Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors

civilization.people: The population should read as Gnomish and city-specific: citizens occupy streets, shops, public thresholds, and landmark approaches as people who understand the local civic order.
socialClasses: laborers, carriers, messengers, and maintenance crews
households tied to local district obligations
civilization.governance: Government is centered on Hanging Charms Veiled High Court. Its authority should be visible through courts, records, public access routes, civic lamps, guards or attendants, and the way citizens respond to D13.
civilization.religion: Religious or ritual life should only be emphasized where source anchors support it, especially names such as Ancestor Vault, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall, Ancestor Masks Monument.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Hearthglen's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Medical / Healing Civic Hall, S2 Arcane / Alchemical Labs Civic Hall, S3 Civic Services Hall, S4 Low Residential Civic Hall, S5 Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter Civic Hall. Keep roads, plazas, stairs, thresholds, lamps, signs-without-readable-text, props, and civilians aligned to the source-backed materials and landmarks.
streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors
exports: specialized tools or ritual goods as supported by anchors
imports: dyes, oils, spices, timber, stone, metal, glass, or cloth as needed by the architecture

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Hanging Charms Veiled High Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
